# Code of Conduct

This is a small project run by one person on side-project time. The
expectations are simple:

- **Be respectful.** Disagree with code and ideas, not with people. No
  personal attacks, slurs, harassment, or trolling.
- **Be patient.** Reviews and merges happen when the maintainer has time.
  Pinging more often will not make them faster.
- **Assume good faith.** Most misunderstandings are misunderstandings, not
  malice. Ask for clarification before escalating.
- **Stay on topic.** Use issues and pull requests for technical discussion.
  Take social, political, or off-topic arguments elsewhere.
- **Don't be a jerk to newcomers.** Everyone was new at something once.

## Enforcement

Violations will be handled at the maintainer's sole discretion. Possible
responses, in roughly increasing severity:

1. A reply asking you to adjust your behavior
2. Editing or removing the offending content
3. Locking a thread
4. Blocking the offender from the repository
5. Reporting to GitHub Trust & Safety, if warranted

There is no formal appeals process. This is a personal project, not a
foundation, and the maintainer is not obligated to debate enforcement
decisions.

## Reporting

If you believe someone is violating this code of conduct, email
**whereiskurt@gmail.com**. Reports are read but acknowledgement is not
guaranteed.

## Scope

This code of conduct applies in all project spaces (issues, pull requests,
discussions, commit messages, related chat channels) and in any public space
where someone is representing the project.

---

This code is intentionally short. It is not the
[Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/) — if you need
that level of formality, this may not be the right project for you.
